title: Dynamic Alerting Platform — Domain Policy Schema (ADR-007)
description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for validating _domain_policy.yaml. Defines business domain compliance constraints applied after the four-layer routing merge. ENFORCED at author/CI time by scripts/tools/lint/check_admin_config_schema.py. NOTE: the runtime parser (components/tenant-api/internal/policy/policy.go) is LENIENT (plain yaml.Unmarshal, no KnownFields), so an unknown constraint key is silently ignored and the constraint never applies — this schema's additionalProperties:false is the ONLY guard against that typo. Because the parser accepts more than it needs, this schema is deliberately kept no STRICTER than its consumers (policy.go + ADR-007 + scripts/tools/lint/check_routing_profiles.py): optional fields stay optional, empty maps/lists are legal, and present-but-null values (which yaml decodes to zero values without error) are accepted.

domain_policies.title: Domain Policies Map
domain_policies.description: Mapping of domain-name to policy configuration. Policies validate (not inject) the final resolved routing config. An empty or null map is legal (no policies configured).

domainPolicy.title: Domain Policy
domainPolicy.description: Business domain compliance constraints. Violations are reported as WARN by default, or ERROR with --strict mode. All fields are optional: policy.go decodes into zero values and a policy that targets no tenant is simply inert.

description.title: Policy Description
description.description: Human-readable description of this domain policy.

tenants.title: Applicable Tenants
tenants.description: List of tenant IDs this policy applies to. A tenant may appear in multiple policies. An empty list means the policy is inert.

constraints.title: Policy Constraints
constraints.description: Validation constraints applied to the tenant's final resolved routing config.

policyConstraints.title: Policy Constraints
policyConstraints.description: Set of validation rules for routing configuration. All constraints are optional; only specified ones are checked. The key set is the union of policy.go's Constraints struct, ADR-007's documented example, and check_routing_profiles.py's valid_constraint_keys.

allowed_receiver_types.title: Allowed Receiver Types
allowed_receiver_types.description: Whitelist of permitted receiver types. If set, any receiver type NOT in this list triggers a violation.

forbidden_receiver_types.title: Forbidden Receiver Types
forbidden_receiver_types.description: Blacklist of prohibited receiver types. Any receiver type in this list triggers a violation.

enforce_group_by.title: Required Group By Labels
enforce_group_by.description: Labels that MUST be present in group_by. Violation if any required label is missing.

max_repeat_interval.title: Maximum Repeat Interval
max_repeat_interval.description: Upper bound for repeat_interval (Go duration). Violation if tenant's repeat_interval exceeds this value. Accepts the common Go-duration forms including fractional units (1.5h) and multi-unit (1h30m); an authoring hint only — policy.go stores the raw string and never ParseDurations it.

min_group_wait.title: Minimum Group Wait
min_group_wait.description: Lower bound for group_wait (Go duration). Violation if tenant's group_wait is below this value. Accepts the common Go-duration forms including fractional units; an authoring hint only — policy.go stores the raw string and never ParseDurations it.

require_critical_escalation.title: Require Critical Escalation
require_critical_escalation.description: Documented in ADR-007 and accepted by check_routing_profiles.py's valid_constraint_keys; the tenant-api Constraints struct does not yet consume it (lenient parse ignores it). Modelled here so a legitimate policy using it is not falsely rejected.
